commit.message:
refactor(api): restructure to RESTful nested routes

Migrate from flat query-param routes to proper REST resource hierarchy:

- PUT    /api/projects/{owner}/{repo}          — add/update project
- DELETE /api/projects/{owner}/{repo}          — remove project
- GET    /api/projects/{owner}/{repo}/metrics  — monthly metrics (public)
- GET    /api/projects/{owner}/{repo}/stars    — star history (public)
- POST   /api/projects/{owner}/{repo}/collect  — start data sync (auth)
- GET    /api/projects/{owner}/{repo}/collect  — reconnect to sync (auth)
- GET    /api/projects                         — list all projects (auth)

Remove old standalone /api/metrics, /api/stars, /api/collect routes.
Update dashboard and chart components to use new endpoints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: board name-search selection now fills project key via follow-up API call

The board list endpoint (/board?name=...) does not return
location.projectKey in its payload for name-search results, so
data-projectkey was always ''.  adminHubOnArtBoardSelected checked
that attribute, found it empty, and did nothing.

Fix: when data-projectkey is empty on the selected option, make a
follow-up GET to /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id} which always includes the
full location object including projectKey.  Fill the project key input
from that response and cache the key on the option element so
re-selecting doesn't re-fetch.

Root cause confirmed via GH issue #16 screenshots: user searched
'Transformers', picked 'Transformers SCRUM (scrum)', Project Key field
remained empty.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ivanoats <4672+ivanoats@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: probe libvips AVIF support to prevent SIGABRT on missing codec

Two compounding bugs caused CI to abort the test process with SIGABRT:

1. The `libvips` Rust crate (1.7.3) has a soundness defect in
   `utils::new_byte_array`: it constructs a `Vec` from a raw pointer
   without a null check. When the C-level `vips_heifsave_buffer` returns
   NULL (which happens whenever the host libvips lacks an AVIF saver,
   e.g. no AV1 encoder linked into libheif), Rust's `core::ub_checks`
   detects the null pointer flowing into `NonNull::new_unchecked` and
   triggers a non-unwinding panic that cannot be caught with
   `catch_unwind`.

2. Default Ubuntu `libvips-dev` is built with libheif support but
   without an AV1 encoder, so `heifsave_buffer_with_opts(compression:
   Av1)` always returns NULL there.

This is a latent production DoS: any user running Rendition on a
libvips without AVIF support would crash their entire process when a
client requested `?fmt=avif`.

Fix: probe AVIF (and WebP) saver availability at first call using
`vips_foreign_find_save_buffer`, a pure capability lookup that does
not invoke the saver itself. Cache the result in a `OnceLock<bool>`.
The encode path now returns a typed error instead of calling
`heifsave_buffer_with_opts` when AVIF is unsupported. The API layer
maps this error to `415 Unsupported Media Type`. AVIF tests probe
support and skip cleanly when unavailable, so CI is green regardless
of which codecs the runner's libvips was built with.

CI also now installs `libheif-dev` and `libaom-dev` best-effort, so
AVIF tests actually run on environments where Ubuntu's libvips picks
them up.

commit.message:
v0.3.16: fix FK constraint in optimizer after parent deletion

After _strip_parents deletes group-master products, their entries
remained in name_to_product. _ensure_parent_product would then
return the stale (deleted) ID, causing FOREIGN KEY constraint
failures when Phase 1 tried to set parent_id.

Fix: purge all old_parent_ids from name_to_product after
_strip_parents so fresh parent products are always created.

commit.message:
fix: only emit components that are needed for css modules at the time of emission (#214)

* fix: restore inline CSS module emission and align inventory with rendered components

The previous PR (#212) moved <style type="module"> emission from inline
(inside each component's light DOM) to a blanket emission in <head> for
all inventoried components, and used document.querySelectorAll + manual
CSSStyleSheet construction for SPA adoption. This had two problems:

1. Bloated SSR HTML with style definitions for every route-reachable
   component, including those not rendered on the current page.
2. document.querySelectorAll cannot pierce shadow DOM boundaries, so
   inline <style type="module"> tags were invisible to the framework.

## What this changes

### SSR: restore inline CSS module emission (handler)

emit_css_module() places the <style type="module" specifier="X"> tag
inside the component's light DOM on first render:

  <my-comp><style type="module" specifier="my-comp">CSS</style><template ...>

The browser registers the CSS module globally from this tag and
automatically adopts it via shadowrootadoptedstylesheets on the
declarative shadow root. No JS needed during SSR hydration.

### SSR: emit templates and inventory for rendered components only

Previously, body_end expanded rendered_components into a "reachable"
set that included components inside unrendered <if>/<for> blocks. This
caused inventory to cover components whose <style type="module">
definitions were never emitted (because emit_css_module only fires for
actually-rendered components), creating a mismatch.

Now body_end emits template IIFEs and builds the inventory from
rendered_components only. Components inside unrendered conditional
blocks are excluded — they arrive via templateStyles[] + templates[]
during SPA partial navigation. This ensures: if a component is in
the inventory, both its template IIFE and its CSS module definition
are in the document.

### SPA: use import() with { type: "css" } for style adoption (framework)

For SPA-created components (no declarative shadow root), the framework
uses the browser's CSS module registry directly:

  import(specifier, { with: { type: "css" } })

This returns the browser-registered CSSStyleSheet — no DOM queries,
no manual CSSStyleSheet construction, no application-level cache.
The browser's module registry is the cache (O(1) hash map lookup).

SSR-hydrated components skip this entirely — the browser already
adopted the sheet from shadowrootadoptedstylesheets.

## Files changed

- **crates/webui-handler/src/lib.rs:** Restore emit_css_module() inline
  emission. Emit templates + inventory from rendered_components only
  (not expanded reachable set). Move inventory meta tag to body_end.
  Remove collect_reachable_components (dead code).
- **packages/webui-framework/src/element/styles.ts:** Replace
  querySelectorAll + new CSSStyleSheet() + sheetCache with
  import(specifier, { with: { type: "css" } }) for SPA path.
  SSR path is a no-op (browser handles adoption).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Feedback

---------

commit.message:
Fix fixable TODOs from TODO.md + add unit test coverage (#2182)

Resolves 29+ auto-triaged "Fix / No human needed" TODOs across the
TypeScript codebase. All changes are local, low-risk improvements — no
architectural changes.

## Dead code
- Removed unused exported `sanitizeKey` from `pdfImporter.ts` and
`pythonImporter.ts`

## SQL (`memoryProviders`)
- `getIds`: replaced per-item lookups with a single `IN` clause query
- `getExactHits`: replaced two-step lookup with an `INNER JOIN` between
postings and text tables
- `vectorTable.put`: implemented auto-ID generation (`lastInsertRowid`
for integer keys, `randomUUID()` for string keys — previously threw
unconditionally)

## CLI
- `spelunker/main.ts`: replaced ad-hoc `process.argv` scanning with
Node.js built-in `parseArgs`
- `commandHistogram`: DB name and collection name read from
`process.env` with hardcoded fallbacks

## Input validation
- `commandTransformer`: validates `result.data` has a string `name`
before dispatching as `NamedArgs`
- Both `pythonChunker` copies: `JSON.parse` output validated against
schema via type guard before use; guard is now exported for testability

## Context window / output formatting (`spelunker`, `docuProc`)
- Context cutoff changed from item count to total character size (100 K
cap)
- Per-blob line budget distributed evenly across blobs
- Code blocks rendered in `chalk.cyan` in word-wrap output

## Refactoring
- `searchCode.ts` (`loadDatabase`): extracted
`getAllSourceFilesFromFolders`, `compareFilesWithDatabase`,
`logChunkerErrors`
- `summarizing.ts`: `languageCommentMap` values changed to `{start,
end}` to support block-comment languages; unused `export` removed
- `spelunker/eval.ts`: hardcoded config replaced with file-based
`eval-config.json` with fallback

## `agents/browser`
- `serviceWorker/index.ts`: RPC args typed via inline
`WebAgentRegisterParam` interface instead of untyped cast
- `entityGraphView`: `getEarliestDate` call site filtered to
`"contains"` relationships only
- `pdfViewer`: implemented text search (`getTextContent` over all
pages), find-next/prev, and download via `getData()` + Blob URL
- `macrosLibrary`: implemented macro editing via dynamically rendered
modal

## `schemaStudio`
- `urlCommands`: redirect + default-parameter handling via
`normalizeUrl` and origin-aware `isRedirectUrl`
- `cacheRESTEndpoint`: action name derived from interface name; schema
fields parsed via AST node traversal instead of regex

## Unit tests added
- `sqlite.vectorTable.spec.ts`: two tests for `put()` without explicit
id (INTEGER auto-increment, TEXT UUID)
- `sqlite.textTable.spec.ts`: two tests explicitly exercising the `IN`
clause (`getIds`) and `INNER JOIN` (`getExactHits`) paths
- `ts/packages/agents/spelunker/test/pythonChunker.spec.ts`: 12 cases
covering all branches of `isChunkedFileOrErrorItemArray` — added jest
infrastructure to the package

commit.message:
Add integration tests for gRPC auth token enforcement over real TCP (#75)

tests/integration/grpc_auth_test.go:

Wire a real gRPC server on a random TCP port to verify auth interceptors
work correctly over the network (not just in-process via bufconn).

- TestIntegration_AuthToken_EmptyTokenSkipsAuth: when AuthToken is empty,
  unauth requests are allowed (local desktop mode).

- TestIntegration_AuthToken_RejectsNoCredentials: when AuthToken is set,
  requests with no Authorization header fail with codes.Unauthenticated.

- TestIntegration_AuthToken_RejectsWrongToken: requests with the wrong
  token fail with codes.Unauthenticated.

- TestIntegration_AuthToken_AllowsCorrectToken: requests with the correct
  "Bearer <token>" header succeed.

- TestIntegration_AuthToken_StreamRejectsNoCredentials: streaming calls
  (GetHistory) also enforce auth and reject unauthenticated requests.

- TestIntegration_AuthToken_StreamAllowsCorrectToken: streaming calls
  accept the correct Bearer token.

- TestIntegration_AuthToken_BearerFormatEnforced: auth header must have
  the "Bearer " prefix; plain tokens without the prefix are rejected.

This layer tests auth over real TCP, whereas internal/server/grpc_test.go
tests auth via bufconn (in-memory pipe). Real TCP catches issues in server
registration order, transport handling, or metadata propagation that might
only surface on the network.

Closes #75

commit.message:
Add integration tests for proxy→storage pipeline (#74)

tests/integration/proxy_storage_test.go:

Wire proxy + engine + storage on a real HTTP proxy (random port) to verify
the core data flow: requests captured by proxy → stored by engine → persisted
in storage.

- TestIntegration_ProxyStoresCorrectFields: sends a real GET through the proxy
  and verifies the RequestRecord has correct URL (with path + query), method,
  headers, plus the ResponseRecord has correct status code and body.

- TestIntegration_ProxyPreservesStatusCodes: sends requests that return 200,
  201, 404, 500 and verifies each stored status code matches exactly.

- TestIntegration_ConcurrentProxyWrites: sends 20 concurrent requests through
  the proxy and verifies all 20+ are stored in the DB with no duplicates.

- TestIntegration_ModifiedRequestStored: demonstrates that breakpoint-modified
  requests are correctly forwarded and stored (modified URL is sent to upstream).

This layer fills the gap between unit tests (which mock components) and E2E tests
(which test through gRPC). No gRPC server started; storage is queried directly.

Closes #74

commit.message:
Add fuzz tests for EnvSubst and breakpoint URL matching (#78)

internal/pluginrt/builtins/builtins_test.go:
- FuzzEnvSubst_Body: fuzzes OnRequest with arbitrary body content;
  seed corpus covers normal placeholders, malformed braces, secret
  variable names, binary data, and empty input. Must not panic.
- FuzzEnvSubst_Headers: fuzzes header name+value pairs through
  OnRequest; catches panics on binary values or placeholder-like
  patterns in header values.

internal/breakpoints/breakpoints_test.go:
- FuzzEvaluate: fuzzes Manager.Evaluate with arbitrary method and
  rawURL strings; covers malformed URLs, null bytes, empty inputs,
  and long strings. The implementation already guards against ReDoS
  via a 100ms timeout; this confirms no other panic paths exist.

All seed corpus entries also run as regular unit tests (go test -run Fuzz)
so CI stays green without a fuzzer enabled.

Run fuzz targets locally:
  go test -fuzz=FuzzEnvSubst_Body    ./internal/pluginrt/builtins/ -fuzztime=60s
  go test -fuzz=FuzzEnvSubst_Headers ./internal/pluginrt/builtins/ -fuzztime=60s
  go test -fuzz=FuzzEvaluate         ./internal/breakpoints/       -fuzztime=60s

Closes #78

feat(ui): add mouse scroll support for Pipeline Stages panel

Route mouse scroll events in the Log Viewer based on cursor position.
When hovering over the tree panel (Pipeline Stages), scroll moves the
timeline selection up/down. When hovering over the log panel, scroll
continues to move the log output as before.

Implementation stores the computed layout Rects on LogViewerState after
each render, then hit-tests mouse.column/row in handle_mouse() to

commit.message:
feat: CI pipeline split, plugin overhaul, CRD validation, manageCniPlugin (#33)

* ci: strip image push from CI, add plugin matrix to release

CI workflow changes:
- Remove push-to-main trigger (CI now runs on PRs and workflow_dispatch only)
- Remove GHCR login step and image push conditionals from build job
  (set push: false always -- CI builds only warm the GHA cache)
- Remove the manifest job (no images to create manifests for)
- Remove image_tags from version job outputs (no longer consumed)
- Drop packages: write permission (only contents: read needed)

Release workflow changes:
- Add a plugin matrix job that builds kubectl-unbounded-net for all
  6 OS/arch combinations in parallel with proper Go build/mod caching,
  replacing the sequential inline build loop in the release job
- Release job now depends on [version, build, manifest, plugin] and
  downloads plugin artifacts instead of building them inline
- Go caching already present on the release job's render-manifests step

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: simplify ZSH completion to not override kubectl

The previous approach hijacked kubectl's compdef, which broke kubectl's
own tab completion and caused OS names (darwin/linux/windows) to appear
as completions. kubectl automatically dispatches completion to plugin
binaries via __complete, so we only need completion for the standalone
kubectl-unbounded-net binary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: overhaul kubectl plugin completion, help, and create commands

Completion:
- Add kubectl_complete-unbounded-net symlink for native kubectl plugin
  tab completion protocol
- Remove completion subcommand (kubectl handles it via the symlink)
- Disable Cobra's default completion command
- Hide inherited kubeconfig flags from completion output
- Add ValidArgsFunction with <name> placeholder for all create commands
- Add active help hints for CIDR, duration, and selector flag values
- Add selectable completions for --type, --dry-run, --output,
  --tunnel-protocol

Help and usage:
- Fix duplicate error output (SilenceErrors)
- Show 'kubectl create <resource>' in usage when invoked via symlink
- Show 'kubectl unbounded net' (no hyphens) to match kubectl display
- Add Long descriptions and Examples to all five create commands
- Indent Long text two spaces in help output
- Add aliases (st, gp, spr, sgpa, gpp) to create subcommands
- Expand flag descriptions with format hints and examples

Symlink management:
- Add kubectl_complete-unbounded-net to symlink create list
- Auto-replace dangling symlinks without --force
- Add -f shorthand for --force

Create command changes:
- Rename --manage-cni-plugin to --no-manage-cni-plugin (default is true)
- Add --pod-cidr-block as required flag
- Add example for sites with existing CNI plugin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: require podCidrAssignments in Site CRD, document manageCniPlugin

CRD validation:
- podCidrAssignments is now required (minItems=1) instead of optional
- cidrBlocks within podCidrAssignments is required (removed omitempty)
- Document that pod CIDR assignment is disabled when manageCniPlugin
  is false, regardless of assignmentEnabled on individual rules

Documentation:
- Update manageCniPlugin behavior table to include Pod CIDR Assignment
- Add explanation of manageCniPlugin interaction with assignmentEnabled
- Add YAML example for sites with an existing CNI plugin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: skip pod CIDR assignment when manageCniPlugin is false

The controller now skips pod CIDR allocation for nodes in sites with
manageCniPlugin set to false. The podCidrAssignments entries are still
required to define the CIDR ranges for inter-site routing, but the
controller will not assign per-node pod CIDRs or modify node specs.

This ensures that sites using an external CNI plugin (e.g. Cilium,
Calico) do not have their pod CIDRs overwritten by the controller.
